1:
A sold-out **Estadio Azteca** witnessed the perfect send-off for the hosts on Thursday, as **Mexico** closed out the group stage with a commanding 2-0 victory over **South Africa** to finish atop **Group A** with a flawless nine points from three wins. Playing in front of their passionate home crowd in Mexico City, El Tri were dominant throughout, sealing their place in the Round of 32 in emphatic fashion and sending a message to the rest of the field.

For **South Africa**, the result stings but doesn't end their tournament hopes — just yet. Bafana Bafana finish second in the group on four points, a record of one win, one draw, and one loss, and a goal difference of -1. That's enough to earn an automatic berth in the Round of 32 as one of the top two finishers in the group, meaning they'll move on alongside the hosts.

**Korea Republic** and **Czechia**, meanwhile, are left waiting anxiously. The Koreans sit third on three points with a goal difference of -1, while **Czechia** prop up the group with just a single point and a -4 goal difference. Whether either team advances depends entirely on how they stack up against the other third-placed sides from around the tournament — only the best eight third-place finishers earn a spot in the Round of 32. Both sides will be glued to results elsewhere as the group stage wraps up across all venues.
2:
A must-win match for both sides turned into a gripping afternoon at **Estadio Akron** in Guadalajara, as **Korea Republic** edged past **Czechia** 2-1 to secure three crucial points in **Group A** and keep their hopes of advancing very much alive.

With **Mexico** having swept through the group with a perfect nine points and **South Africa** sitting on four points ahead of kickoff, the math was unforgiving for both teams. **Korea Republic** needed the win badly, and they delivered, claiming their first victory of the group stage after back-to-back defeats. **Czechia**, meanwhile, suffered their second loss in three matches, leaving them with just a single point and on the verge of an early exit.

The result sees **Korea Republic** finish third in **Group A** on three points with a goal difference of -1, meaning their fate is no longer in their own hands. They must now wait and see whether their record is good enough to be among the eight best third-placed teams from across the tournament — a tense watch that will play out over the coming days. **South Africa** claim second place on four points and join **Mexico** in advancing automatically to the Round of 32. **Czechia** finish bottom of the group on one point and are eliminated from Football 2026, their tournament ending at the group stage.
3:
A tense afternoon at **BMO Field** in Toronto ended with both sides sharing the spoils, as **Canada** and **Bosnia and Herzegovina** played out a 1-1 draw to close out Group B — a result that left the hosts breathing easy but only just. With **Switzerland** already through as group winners on 7 points, it was the second automatic berth that was up for grabs, and **Canada** held on to it, finishing level on 4 points with **Bosnia and Herzegovina** but edging them out on goal difference in what turned out to be a nail-biting final reckoning.

The draw means **Canada**, in their first appearance at this level in 36 years, advance to the Round of 32 as group runners-up — a moment that will resonate deeply with a home crowd that packed BMO Field hoping for exactly this outcome. **Bosnia and Herzegovina**, also finishing on 4 points, drop to third in Group B with a goal difference of -1, and their fate now rests in the hands of the broader standings: they will need to rank among the eight best third-placed teams across all groups to survive into the knockout rounds.

**Qatar**, meanwhile, finish bottom of the group on just 1 point with a goal difference of -8, their tournament over. For the Canadians, the relief of qualifying on home soil will give way quickly to preparation for their Round of 32 fixture — details on their opponent, timing, and venue still to be confirmed once all group stage results are in. For **Bosnia and Herzegovina**, the wait begins.
4:
A raucous home crowd at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood got exactly what they came for, as **USA** dismantled **Paraguay** 4-1 to finish top of **Group D** and punch their ticket to the Round of 32. The result ends any suspense in the group standings, with the Americans claiming six points from their three matches and posting a goal difference of +4 to seal first place in convincing fashion.

The hosts were in control from early on, and the margin of victory left little room for debate about who deserved to lead the group. **Paraguay** had arrived with something to play for but leave Inglewood with their tournament fate now hinging on the third-place standings — their 4-1 defeat alongside a 1W-1D-1L record and a goal difference of -2 puts them level on points with **Australia** but behind on goal difference. Whether that's enough to claim one of the eight best third-place spots remains to be seen, depending on results elsewhere across the tournament.

**Australia**, meanwhile, also advances automatically as runners-up on four points, earning a place in the Round of 32 alongside the Americans. **Türkiye** exits the tournament after finishing fourth with just three points and the same goal difference of -2 as Paraguay, unable to recover from a difficult group campaign. As for the USA, they move on as group winners and will await their Round of 32 opponent — details to be confirmed once all group stage results are in. For now, the home faithful at SoFi Stadium can savor a dominant performance that sent a message to the rest of the field.
5:
A hard-fought 1-0 victory for **Scotland** over **Haiti** at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough wrapped up Group C with a late twist in the standings. The Scots claimed all three points in what was effectively a dead rubber for both sides heading into the final matchday — **Haiti** already eliminated and **Scotland** needing a win to salvage any hope of advancing — and they got the job done with a single goal that proved just enough to send them into the third-place reckoning.

The result leaves **Scotland** on 3 points with a goal difference of -3, finishing third in **Group C** behind **Brazil** and **Morocco**, who both advance automatically on 7 points apiece after dominant group stage campaigns. For **Haiti**, it ends in heartbreak — three losses from three, no points, and a goal difference of -6 marking a brutal introduction to the tournament for the Caribbean nation.

Whether **Scotland**'s third-place finish is enough to see them through now depends entirely on results elsewhere. They will need to rank among the top eight third-placed teams across all groups, and with a -3 goal difference, it will be a nervous wait. **Brazil** and **Morocco**, meanwhile, have already secured their spots in the Round of 32 and will learn their knockout-stage opponents once the full group stage picture is complete. For **Haiti**, the journey ends here — but their first-ever appearance at Football 2026 will not be forgotten quickly.
6:
A must-win became a mission accomplished for **Australia**, who shut out **Türkiye** 2-0 at BC Place in Vancouver to secure their place in the Round of 32 and send their opponents to the brink of elimination from **Group D**. The Socceroos were the sharper side throughout, doing just enough to climb to four points and second place in the final group standings — a position they'll gladly take heading into the knockout rounds.

The result leaves **Türkiye** with an anxious wait to see whether their three points and a goal difference of -2 is enough to sneak into the top eight third-placed finishers across all groups. Having won just one of their three group matches, the road back from Vancouver ends here if the numbers don't fall their way — a bitter outcome for a side that arrived with genuine ambitions of a deep run.

With all **Group D** matches now complete, **USA** top the table on six points following a dominant group stage, while **Australia** join them in the automatic qualification spots. **Paraguay** also finish on four points but drop to third on goal difference, leaving them sweating alongside **Türkiye** on the third-place bubble. **Australia** will now turn their attention to the Round of 32, where they'll learn their next opponent as the bracket takes shape — a chance to prove their narrow qualification was just the beginning of something bigger at Football 2026.
7:
A dramatic draw at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford ensured **Group C** went down to the wire, as **Brazil** and **Morocco** played out a tense 1-1 stalemate that settled very little at the top of the table. The two sides finish level on seven points apiece — both unbeaten through three matches — leaving the tiebreakers to decide seeding rather than qualification, with both nations already assured of their spots in the Round of 32.

The result means **Brazil** top the group on goal difference, their +6 edging out **Morocco**'s +3 to hand the Seleção the top seed heading into the knockouts. It is the first time **Brazil** have been held in the group stage since the 2018 edition of the tournament, a run of seven consecutive group-stage wins finally halted by a **Morocco** side that has now gone unbeaten in their last five tournament matches stretching back to their historic run in 2022. **Scotland** finish third on three points, still clinging to hopes of sneaking through as one of the eight best third-placed sides, while **Haiti** bow out pointless after three defeats, outscored by a combined margin of six goals.

Both **Brazil** and **Morocco** will now await confirmation of their Round of 32 opponents once the remaining group-stage matches are complete, with the bracket set to be finalized in the coming days. For **Scotland**, the wait continues — their fate rests entirely on results elsewhere across Football 2026.
8:
A hard-fought 1-1 draw at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara saw **Qatar** salvage a point against **Switzerland** in the final round of **Group B** action, but it wasn't enough to save the host nation from an early exit. The result ends **Qatar**'s tournament on just one point from three matches — a painful conclusion for a side that had the weight of a nation's expectations on their shoulders on foreign soil for the very first time.

**Switzerland**, meanwhile, finish top of **Group B** with seven points and a thoroughly deserved place in the Round of 32. The draw did little to dampen their momentum, with the Swiss having already done the hard work in their first two matches. They advance as group winners and will await their next assignment in the knockout rounds.

The bigger drama, however, surrounds **Canada** and **Bosnia and Herzegovina**, who both finish on four points and must now sweat on their fate. Both sides are done playing, but with eight third-placed teams across the expanded tournament also set to advance, the door remains open. **Canada** boast a superior goal difference of +5 compared to **Bosnia and Herzegovina**'s -1, which could prove decisive if either side finds themselves slipping into third-place contention — though both finish second and third respectively here and will be watching results elsewhere with nervous eyes as the group stage concludes across the tournament.
9:
A hard-fought afternoon at **Lincoln Financial Field** in Philadelphia ended with **Côte d'Ivoire** edging out **Ecuador** 1-0 in the final round of **Group E** fixtures, a result that carried enormous consequences for both sides. The Elephants' narrow victory was enough to see them through to the Round of 32 alongside **Germany**, who had already secured top spot heading into matchday three.

With the group now fully decided, **Côte d'Ivoire** finish second on six points — level with **Germany** but separated by goal difference — having bounced back impressively after their opening defeat. **Ecuador** bow out on four points, a tally that may have been enough in another group but ultimately left them on the wrong side of the line here. Their slim hopes of sneaking through as one of the eight best third-placed teams were extinguished by the final whistle. **Curaçao**, meanwhile, finish bottom with just a single point from their three matches and are also eliminated.

The Elephants now turn their attention to the knockout rounds, where they'll face an opponent to be determined once all group stage results are tallied. **Germany**, finishing as Group E winners, will also await their Round of 32 assignment. For **Ecuador**, a campaign that had shown genuine promise — including a draw and a win earlier in the group — ends here in Philadelphia, one result short of the knockout stage they had every reason to believe they could reach.
10:
A stunning demolition in the Houston heat settled **Group E** with a bang, as **Germany** thrashed **Curaçao** 7-1 at NRG Stadium to finish the group stage in ruthless, emphatic fashion. The result confirmed the Germans as group winners on six points, though the night's biggest storyline was the sheer scale of the victory — the seven-goal haul pushed their goal difference to +6 and sent a sharp warning to the rest of the tournament.

**Curaçao**, who had shown enough heart in earlier group matches to earn a single point, were simply overwhelmed from the opening whistle. The Caribbean side finish bottom of the group on one point with a goal difference of -8, and their debut campaign at Football 2026 ends here. For a nation appearing on this stage for the first time, there were moments to be proud of across the group, but nothing could paper over the chasm in class on display at NRG Stadium on Sunday night.

With all Group E matches now complete, **Germany** and **Côte d'Ivoire** both advance on six points — separated only by goal difference — into the Round of 32, while **Ecuador** will sweat on results elsewhere, sitting on four points as they wait to see whether they rank among the eight best third-placed teams across the tournament. The Germans will take enormous confidence into the knockouts, having looked every bit the title contenders many expected them to be. Their Round of 32 opponent, date, time, and venue will be confirmed once the full group stage picture is settled.
11:
A dramatic 2-2 draw at AT&T Stadium in Arlington settled **Group F** in chaotic, breathless fashion, as **Japan** refused to let **Netherlands** coast to a comfortable group win. The Dutch came in already assured of advancement and needing little more than a point to finish top of the group, and despite twice taking the lead, they were twice pegged back by a resilient Japanese side that simply would not lie down.

The result leaves **Netherlands** atop Group F on 7 points, finishing as group winners and securing a place in the Round of 32. **Japan** end in second on 5 points, also advancing — their first progression past the group stage since 2010. **Sweden**, on 4 points, finish third and will need to sweat on results elsewhere, hoping their goal difference holds up as one of the eight best third-placed finishers across all groups. **Tunisia** exit winless after three matches, bowing out with a -10 goal difference that made for painful reading.

With all of Group F now complete, attention turns to the knockout rounds. **Netherlands** and **Japan** will learn their Round of 32 opponents once the group stage concludes, with both nations now guaranteed a place in the next phase of Football 2026. For the Dutch, finishing first means a potentially more favorable draw; for **Japan**, the result underlined a tournament mentality built on grit and belief. **Sweden**'s fate hangs on the performances of third-placed finishers across the bracket — a nervy wait begins.
12:
A dominant display at the **Estadio BBVA** in Monterrey saw **Sweden** dismantle **Tunisia** 5-1 in the final round of **Group F** fixtures, but it wasn't enough to save their knockout stage hopes. The Swedes produced their best performance of the tournament on the day it mattered least for them, running riot against an already-eliminated Tunisian side that finishes bottom of the group without a single point from three matches, conceding ten goals along the way.

The result lifts **Sweden** to four points and a third-place finish in **Group F**, but whether that's enough depends entirely on what happens elsewhere. With the top eight third-placed finishers across all groups advancing to the Round of 32, the Swedes now face an anxious wait as the remaining group stage matches play out. Their goal difference of zero — boosted significantly by this thrashing — gives them a fighting chance, but they are far from guaranteed a spot in the knockouts.

Up at the top of the group, **Netherlands** cruise through as winners on seven points, with **Japan** joining them in second on five. The Dutch and the Japanese can start planning for the Round of 32, their progression long since secured before this final matchday. For **Tunisia**, it's a brutal group stage exit — three losses, ten goals conceded, none of the fight that neutrals might have hoped for. **Sweden**, meanwhile, must wait and watch, clinging to the hope that their five-goal haul on a sunny afternoon in Monterrey proves just enough to keep their tournament alive.
13:
A dramatic late equalizer at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens ensured **Group H** ended with a whimper rather than a bang, as **Saudi Arabia** and **Uruguay** played out a 1-1 draw that left both sides sweating over their tournament futures — though ultimately unable to change their fate.

**Uruguay** had taken the lead and looked set to leapfrog into a more comfortable position, but **Saudi Arabia** hit back to level and leave both teams stranded on two points. With **Spain** having already wrapped up top spot on seven points and **Cabo Verde** finishing second on three, the real drama was happening in the standings rather than on the pitch. Neither **Uruguay** nor **Saudi Arabia** managed to overhaul the Cape Verdeans, leaving both sides to finish third and fourth respectively — separated only by goal difference, with **Uruguay** holding a slight edge at -1 compared to **Saudi Arabia**'s -4.

Both teams are now eliminated from automatic advancement but are not yet certain to be going home. **Uruguay** finish as one of the third-placed sides across all groups, meaning they will be assessed against the other third-place finishers from Football 2026's expanded format — the best eight of those teams advance to the Round of 32. **Saudi Arabia**, meanwhile, are almost certainly out, as their goal difference makes them one of the weaker third-place finishers in the field. **Spain** advance directly as group winners, while **Cabo Verde** — in one of the tournament's early surprises — punch their ticket to the knockouts as runners-up.
14:
A hard-fought stalemate at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta saw **Spain** and **Cabo Verde** play out a goalless draw, a result that nonetheless left both sides with reason to celebrate when the final whistle blew. **Spain** had already secured top spot in **Group H** heading into the match, and the draw confirmed they finish on seven points — unbeaten through three matches with a goal difference of plus-five. **Cabo Verde**, meanwhile, earned their third consecutive draw of the group stage and clung onto second place on three points, enough to see them through to the Round of 32.

It was a cagey, low-stakes affair by the standards of a tournament group finale, with **Spain** rotating liberally and **Cabo Verde** content to sit deep and protect what they had. The islanders, making just their second-ever appearance at this level of international football, became the first team in tournament history to advance from a group stage without winning a single match — three draws getting the job done in a crowded and competitive **Group H**.

**Uruguay** and **Saudi Arabia** both finished on two points, leaving them reliant on the overall third-place standings to keep their hopes alive — though neither side's goal difference will make for comfortable reading in the coming days. As for the group winners, **Spain** advance in commanding fashion and will face a Round of 32 opponent yet to be determined once all group play concludes. **Cabo Verde** join them in the knockouts, a remarkable achievement for a nation still writing its footballing story.
15:
A dramatic late leveler at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood ensured **IR Iran** and **New Zealand** shared the spoils in a 2-2 draw, a result that brought the curtain down on **Group G** with both sides knowing their fate heading into the knockout rounds.

**Iran** finish third in the group with three points and a flat goal difference of zero, having gone the entire group stage without a win — three draws, no victories, no defeats. Whether that's enough to carry them into the Round of 32 depends entirely on how the third-placed teams across the other groups shake out; they'll be sweating on results elsewhere as the best eight third-place finishers earn a reprieve. **Belgium** and **Egypt** — level on five points apiece but separated by goal difference — claimed the top two spots and both advance automatically to the Round of 32. **New Zealand**, meanwhile, finish bottom of the group on just a single point from their three matches, a goal difference of -6 proving too steep a hill to climb, and they are eliminated from Football 2026.

For **New Zealand** this was a spirited farewell but ultimately a tournament too far. **Iran**, by contrast, leave SoFi Stadium with a faint pulse — unbeaten, but never quite convincing enough to put the group away. Their fate is now out of their hands, and they'll spend the coming days watching and waiting as the rest of the group stage concludes across the continent.
16:
A tense and tightly contested draw at **Lumen Field** in Seattle saw **Belgium** and **Egypt** play out a 1-1 stalemate that, remarkably, sent both sides through to the Round of 32 with identical records. With all Group G matches now complete, the two nations finish level on five points apiece — **Belgium** claiming top spot on goal difference (+4 to **Egypt**'s +2) — in one of the more evenly balanced groups of the tournament.

The match had the feel of two sides playing for position rather than survival, though both goals were anything but timid. **Egypt** drew first blood before **Belgium** leveled to ensure neither team would blink first in the final standings. **IR Iran**, who remarkably went unbeaten across all three group matches without ever winning one, finish third on three points and will need to hope their goal difference holds up among the best third-placed sides. **New Zealand** exit at the group stage after collecting just one point.

Both **Belgium** and **Egypt** now await their Round of 32 opponents, drawn from the other qualifying groups. With the Red Devils pipping **Egypt** to first place, the two sides will face different paths through the bracket — a small but potentially significant reward for **Belgium**'s superior goal difference across the group. **IR Iran**'s fate, meanwhile, rests with results elsewhere, as they sit among the third-placed sides hoping to scrape through in what is shaping up to be a fiercely competitive race for the final eight spots in that category.
17:
A dominant **France** side wrapped up Group I with a perfect nine points, cruising to a 3-1 win over **Senegal** at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford to finish the group stage unbeaten and in imperious form. The result confirmed Les Bleus as the top seeds heading into the Round of 32, with a goal difference of +8 across three matches making them one of the most convincing group winners of the tournament so far.

**Senegal** had plenty to play for but ultimately couldn't live with France's quality on the night. The Lions of Teranga finish third in Group I on three points, their fate now hinging on whether their goal difference of +2 is enough to squeeze into the best eight third-placed finishers and earn a spot in the Round of 32. It's an anxious wait for Aliou Cissé's side as results elsewhere come in — they're in the mix, but far from safe.

**Iraq**, meanwhile, exit the tournament winless after three matches, finishing bottom of Group I with zero points and a goal difference of -11. **Norway** claimed second place on six points, and both Norway and France now advance automatically to the Round of 32, joining the bracket as two of the stronger sides in this half of the draw. France's next match date, time, and venue will be confirmed once the full third-place standings are resolved and the Round of 32 bracket is set — but based on their form at MetLife Stadium, few teams will be eager to draw them.
18:
A commanding performance at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough saw **Norway** dismantle **Iraq** 4-1 on Tuesday, wrapping up **Group I** with a statement win that secured their place in the Round of 32. The Norwegians, who entered the match needing a result after an earlier stumble in the group, delivered emphatically — four goals to the good and never seriously threatened after Iraq pulled one back to make momentary noise on the scoreboard.

It was a result that cemented Norway in second place behind a dominant **France** side that swept the group with a perfect nine points. **Iraq**, meanwhile, finished bottom of the group with zero points from three matches and a brutal goal difference of -11, bringing their Football 2026 campaign to an end in the United States having been unable to find the answers against the tournament's stronger sides.

With all Group I matches now complete, France and Norway advance, while Iraq are eliminated. Norway's reward is a place in the Round of 32, where they'll await the completion of other groups to learn their opponent — one of the top two finishers from a paired group, or potentially a third-placed qualifier. **France** top the group on nine points with a goal difference of +8, setting up what looks like a deep run for one of the tournament's genuine contenders. For Norway, it's job done — survive the group, and now turn attention to what comes next in the knockout rounds.
19:
A commanding performance at **Arrowhead Stadium** in Kansas City sealed a perfect group stage for **Argentina**, who swept aside **Algeria** 3-0 to finish **Group J** with a flawless nine points from nine — three wins, no dropped points, and a goal difference that stands at +7.

The result leaves little doubt about where the tournament favorites in this group stood throughout. **Argentina** were clinical from the first whistle, their clinical finishing and defensive solidity making the outcome look inevitable long before the final whistle. For **Algeria**, the loss stings but doesn't end their tournament — they finish third in the group on four points alongside **Austria**, who also close out on four points and a level goal difference. **Austria** take second place by virtue of a superior head-to-head or goals scored tally, meaning they advance automatically. **Algeria's** fate now hinges on the broader standings across all groups — they'll be sweating on results elsewhere to see whether they rank among the best eight third-placed teams and sneak into the Round of 32.

**Jordan**, meanwhile, finish bottom of the group with zero points from three matches and are eliminated. As for **Argentina**, they march into the knockouts as group winners and will await their Round of 32 opponent once the full group stage picture is confirmed — a prospect that will concern whoever draws the short straw of facing the reigning champions.
20:
A must-win clash at **Levi's Stadium** in Santa Clara delivered exactly what **Austria** needed, as they ground out a 3-1 victory over already-eliminated **Jordan** to secure their spot in the Round of 32 from **Group J**. The Austrians came into the evening knowing that anything less than a win could see them leapfrogged by **Algeria**, and they left the Bay Area venue with their bags packed for the knockout rounds, finishing second in the group behind a dominant **Argentina** side that swept all three of their matches.

**Jordan**, who exit the tournament without a single point from three matches — falling to Argentina, Algeria, and now Austria — were unable to offer much resistance once the tone was set. The 3-1 scoreline flatters neither side particularly, but it was enough to send **Austria** through with four points, edging **Algeria** on goal difference after the two sides finished level on points. The final margin of +2 proved just enough separation when the numbers were crunched across the group.

With the group now fully settled, **Austria** turn their attention to the Round of 32, where the stakes rise sharply. They will await the outcome of the best third-placed team selections before their next fixture is confirmed, but the belief will be high after finding their groove at just the right moment. For **Jordan**, the tournament ends as a sobering but valuable education on football's biggest stage — three defeats, five goals conceded, and lessons to carry into future qualifying campaigns.
21:
A hard-fought afternoon at **BMO Field** in Toronto saw **Ghana** grind out a 1-0 victory over **Panama** to secure third place in **Group L** — though whether that's enough to keep their tournament alive remains to be seen. The Black Stars did what they needed to do, picking up three points to finish on four and hand the Panamanians a third consecutive defeat to send them home without a single point to their name.

**Panama** arrived already eliminated and with nothing to play for, and it showed. **Ghana**'s winning goal was enough to edge them past a side that struggled to threaten throughout, finishing the group stage with a goal difference of -4 and a tournament to forget. For the Canaleros, this was a harsh lesson in the gap between CONCACAF qualification and the demands of the global stage.

For **Ghana**, the wait now begins. The Black Stars finish third in **Group L** on four points with a goal difference of zero, and their fate hinges on how they stack up against the other third-placed finishers across all the groups. Eight of those sides will advance to the Round of 32, and **Ghana** will be watching the scoreboards closely as the remaining group matches play out. **England** top the group on seven points, with **Croatia** close behind on six — both advancing automatically — but for **Ghana**, it's a nervous wait to find out whether this win was enough to extend their Football 2026 journey.
22:
A pulsating clash at AT&T Stadium in Arlington settled **Group L** in emphatic fashion, with **England** running out 4-2 winners over **Croatia** to finish top of the group and send both sides through to the Round of 32. The result gives England seven points from their three games, while Croatia finish second on six — a final-day defeat that stings but ultimately doesn't cost them.

England were relentless, building a commanding lead before Croatia gave the scoreline a more respectable look late on. The margin of victory lifts the Three Lions to a group-best goal difference of +4, confirming their status as the dominant force in Group L after three matches. Croatia, who had won their opening two games, will be left to rue a performance that fell well short of their earlier showings, though Zlatko Dalić's side can take comfort in knowing their place in the knockouts was never truly in danger.

With the group now fully decided, **Ghana** finish third on four points — their fate resting on whether that haul is enough to squeeze into the best eight third-place finishers across all groups. **Panama** bow out without a point, a group-stage exit that underlines the vast gulf between the tournament's established nations and its newest arrivals. England advance to the Round of 32 as group winners and will face the third-place finisher from another group, while Croatia slot in as runners-up for their own last-32 assignment — match details for both sides to be confirmed once all groups conclude.
23:
A tense evening at NRG Stadium in Houston ended in stalemate as **Portugal** and **Congo DR** played out a 1-1 draw in **Group K**, a result that leaves both sides' knockout fates confirmed — though by very different margins.

**Portugal** finish second in the group on five points, held back only by **Colombia**'s dominant seven-point haul at the top. The draw was enough to secure their place in the Round of 32, with Cristiano Ronaldo and company unable to claim the group but safely through regardless. **Congo DR**, meanwhile, end on four points in third place, and now face an anxious wait to discover whether that tally is enough to squeeze into the tournament's best eight third-placed teams — a route that could yet extend their remarkable debut run in the competition.

Bottom side **Uzbekistan** finished their group campaign without a single point, eliminated after three defeats that left them with a goal difference of -9, the worst in the group by some distance. With all **Group K** matches now complete, attention turns to what comes next: **Portugal** will learn their Round of 32 opponent once the full picture of third-placed finishers is settled, while **Congo DR** can only watch and wait. For now, Houston's NRG Stadium delivered exactly the kind of nervy, competitive football a do-or-die group closer demands — neither side willing to hand the other an inch, and neither getting quite what they wanted.
24:
A dominant second-half display sent **Colombia** surging to the top of **Group K**, as they put **Uzbekistan** to the sword 3-1 at the iconic Estadio Azteca in Mexico City to close out the group stage in convincing fashion. The Colombians finish with seven points from three matches, securing their place in the Round of 32 and sending a clear message to the rest of the tournament that they mean business.

**Uzbekistan**, meanwhile, endured a miserable group stage campaign, finishing bottom of the table with zero points and a goal difference of -9 across three matches — the heaviest aggregate defeat of any side in Group K. Their lone consolation on the night came too little, too late, a moment of pride in an otherwise sobering introduction to the expanded tournament format.

With all Group K matches now complete, **Portugal** claim the second automatic qualifying spot on five points, while **Congo DR** finish third on four points and will now anxiously await the final group stage results from across the competition to see whether their tally is enough to squeeze into one of the eight best third-place berths. **Colombia**, fresh off their group-winning performance at the Azteca, will turn their attention to the Round of 32 — details of their next fixture to be confirmed once the full group stage picture is settled. For **Uzbekistan**, the journey ends here, their first major tournament appearance at this level proving a steep and unforgiving learning curve.
25:
A point apiece at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta was enough for **South Africa** but ultimately not enough for **Czechia**, as the two sides played out a tense 1-1 draw in the final round of **Group A** fixtures on June 18. 73:
History was made at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood on Sunday as **Canada** edged **South Africa** 1-0 in the Round of 32 — not just a victory for the Canadians, but the opening match of the tournament's expanded knockout format, the first-ever Round of 32 game in Football 2026 history. Both nations were making their debut in a competitive knockout match, and it was Canada who seized the moment, grinding out a result that sends them through to the last 16.

The match carried a quiet but significant weight of history. **South Africa**, who famously hosted the tournament in 2010 without advancing past the group stage, were looking to finally break through in the knockouts — but it wasn't to be. The two sides had met just once before, a 2-0 **South Africa** friendly win back in 2007, and their first-ever competitive encounter ended in heartbreak for Bafana Bafana, who are now eliminated from Football 2026.

**Canada** advance and will face the winner of **Brazil** vs **Japan** on Saturday, July 4 at 10:00 AM PT at NRG Stadium in Houston. It's a daunting potential next assignment, but the Canadians will have earned every right to dream after navigating their way through this tense, historic encounter at SoFi.
74:
Twelve years on from lifting the trophy, **Germany**'s return to knockout football ended in heartbreak at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough, as **Paraguay** held their nerve to win 4-3 on penalties after a 1-1 draw through 120 minutes in the Round of 32. The Germans had been eliminated in the group stage at both 2018 and 2022, and their first knockout appearance since that 2014 final felt like a genuine moment of resurgence — only to dissolve in a shootout that sent the Paraguayans through.

For all **Germany**'s attacking balance in the group stage — ten goals from seven different scorers, including a 7-1 demolition of Curaçao — they could not break down a **Paraguay** side that Julian Nagelsmann himself had warned was "uncomfortable." Gustavo Alfaro's team posted the second-lowest xG of any of the 48 nations during the group stage, yet they defended with exactly the compactness Nagelsmann had feared. Depleted at the back without the injured Nico Schlotterbeck, Germany also became the first side to concede in nine consecutive tournament matches since their own run from 1934 to 1954 — an unwanted slice of history. Miguel Almirón served his suspension after picking up the tournament's first-ever red card for the new rule on covering one's mouth, yet **Paraguay** hardly missed him.

**Paraguay**'s last knockout run came at the 2010 tournament, when they beat Japan on penalties in this same round before bowing out to Spain — and now they are back in the Round of 16. They will face the winner of **Netherlands** vs **Morocco** on Saturday, July 4 at 2:00 PM PT at Lincoln Financial Field in Philadelphia. **Germany**, meanwhile, are eliminated.
75:
Thirty-two years on from their last meeting at a tournament held on American soil, **Netherlands** and **Morocco** could not be separated at the Estadio BBVA in Monterrey, playing out a 1-1 draw that sends both sides into the Round of 16. The symmetry was hard to miss: on June 29, 1994, a Netherlands side that included a young Ronald Koeman beat **Morocco** 2-1 in the group stage of USA '94 — now, with Koeman in the dugout rather than on the pitch, his team had to settle for a share of the spoils.

The subplot of three Morocco players born in the Netherlands — Sofyan Amrabat, Noussair Mazraoui, and Anass Salah-Eddine, the latter two having come through Dutch youth setups — gave the occasion an extra edge, and it was Ismael Saibari who kept his remarkable run going for the Moroccan side, netting for the fourth consecutive match of the tournament to level proceedings. The Atlas Lions, who stunned the world by reaching the semi-finals in 2022, showed once again that they are no easy out, while the three-time finalists from the **Netherlands** were reminded that a trophy remains stubbornly out of reach for a nation that has come so close so many times.

Both sides advance to the Round of 16, where their paths will diverge. Details on their next opponents, times, and venues are to be confirmed as the rest of the bracket settles — but after this battle at Estadio BBVA, neither team will be short of motivation heading into the knockout rounds.
76:
Houston's NRG Stadium was the stage for one of the most eagerly anticipated clashes of the Round of 32, and **Brazil** made sure the occasion didn't get away from them, edging out a determined **Japan** side 2-1 to advance to the Round of 16. Vinícius Jr was once again at the heart of it, continuing a run of form that has seen him join a legendary roll call — Jairzinho, Romário, Ronaldo, and Rivaldo — as the only Brazilians to score in all three group matches at a single tournament. Without the injured Raphinha, Brazil needed their talisman to deliver, and he obliged.

**Japan**, for their part, refused to make it easy. The Blue Samurai arrived in Houston without Takefusa Kubo, Wataru Endo, and Kaoru Mitoma — a remarkable number of absentees for a side that had gone unbeaten through Group F — and they made a genuine fist of it, pulling one back to set up a nervy finish. The result, however, denied them the historic moment they craved: all four of **Japan**'s previous knockout appearances have ended in elimination, including penalty shootout exits to Paraguay in 2010 and Croatia in 2022, and despite a stunning comeback win over **Brazil** in a friendly as recently as October 2025, they couldn't replicate that magic on the grandest stage. They are eliminated.

**Brazil** advance and will face **Canada** on Saturday, July 4 at 10:00 AM PT, back at NRG Stadium in Houston. Their only previous tournament meeting with **Japan** — a 4-1 group stage win in Dortmund in 2006 — now has a companion result, and the five-time champions will be hoping their momentum carries into a heavyweight North American derby.
77:
A dominant **France** side swept **Sweden** aside 3-0 in the Round of 32 at MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford, NJ, confirming their status as one of the tournament's most complete outfits and booking their place in the last 16. The result was no surprise from a French team that won all three of their group matches — one of just three sides alongside **Argentina** and **Mexico** to navigate the group stage with a perfect record.

Didier Deschamps, returning to the touchline after missing the Norway match following the death of his mother, would have liked what he saw. The tournament's most dangerous creative force, Michael Olise, once again pulled the strings, adding to his tournament-leading tally of three assists and seven through balls — numbers no other player at Football 2026 can match. **Sweden**, meanwhile, were unable to reproduce the kind of performance that carried them through the group stage, and their long wait for a knockout win stretches on. The Swedes' only victory in the knockout rounds since beating **Switzerland** in 2018 remains elusive, and their tournament is over.

**France** now turn their attention to one of the blockbuster fixtures of the round of 16: a meeting with **Brazil** on Sunday, July 5 at 1:00 PM PT, back at MetLife Stadium. For Deschamps' side, the momentum is real — Ousmane Dembélé's extraordinary hat-trick against **Norway**, scored from an xG of just 0.97 and the second-fastest in tournament history, served notice that this squad is capable of moments of genuine brilliance. They will need it against the Brazilians.
78:
A historic night at AT&T Stadium in Arlington ended in heartbreak for **Côte d'Ivoire**, who fell 2-1 to **Norway** in the Round of 32 and were eliminated from the tournament. It was nonetheless a landmark moment for the Ivorians — their first-ever knockout appearance, something the legendary generation of Didier Drogba, Yaya Touré, and Kolo Touré never achieved across three tournaments in 2006, 2010, and 2014. This young squad, the youngest at Football 2026 with an average age of just 25.4, gave everything, but ultimately couldn't contain a Norwegian side with the most dangerous striker on the planet.

Erling Haaland, fully rested after sitting out **Norway**'s final group stage match against France, needed no warm-up period. The man who arrived at the knockouts with four goals already in two matches continued to defy arithmetic — his 59 goals in 52 international caps represent the best goals-per-game ratio of any player with 50 or more international goals in the last century. **Côte d'Ivoire** grabbed one back to make it nervy, but Norway held on to seal a famous win.

It is **Norway**'s first knockout victory since 1998 — a 28-year wait that included a Round of 16 exit to Italy that same year. They advance to the Round of 16 and will face Morocco on Sunday, July 5 at 1:00 PM PT at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey. **Côte d'Ivoire**, meanwhile, depart with their heads held high, having finally broken through to the knockout stage — a foundation this youthful side will hope to build on for years to come.
79:
A packed Estadio Azteca got exactly what it came for as **Mexico** dispatched **Ecuador** 2-0 in the Round of 32, sending the host nation through to the last 16 and breathing new life into a fanbase that has been haunted for decades by the so-called "fifth game curse" — seven consecutive tournament exits at the round of 16 stretching back to 1994. This new expanded format means Mexico never even had to navigate that particular stage to get here, but the weight of history hung over every minute regardless, and the crowd made sure the players felt every last bit of it.

True to form, **Mexico** kept their defensive sheet clean through the first 45 minutes — a run that now extends to 15 consecutive tournament matches without conceding a first-half goal, a streak dating all the way back to 2014. **Ecuador**, for their part, were unable to rewrite their own painful knockout history. Their only previous appearance at this stage ended in a 1-0 loss to **England** at the 2006 tournament, and they depart Football 2026 still without a single knockout-round victory to their name, eliminated after falling short on the biggest stage once again.

**Mexico** advance and will remain at home, returning to the Estadio Azteca on Sunday, July 5 at 5:00 PM PT to face the winner of **England** vs **Congo DR** — a mouthwatering potential rematch with the side that ended **Ecuador**'s tournament run twenty years ago, and a game that could finally, genuinely, push El Tri into territory they have never reached in the modern era.
80:
A historic night for **Congo DR** at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta ultimately ended in heartbreak, as **England** edged past the African side 2-1 in the Round of 32 to book their place in the last 16. It was a momentous occasion for the Congolese, making their first-ever knockout appearance at the tournament — their only previous appearance coming as Zaire in 1974, when they lost all three group stage matches and went home without scoring a single goal. They pushed **England** all the way this time, but it wasn't enough.

The Three Lions were forced to navigate the tie without a fully fit defensive unit, missing RB Reece James (hamstring) and CB Jarell Quansah (ankle), with deputy option Tino Livramento also unavailable after a calf injury ruled him out before the tournament began. Still, **England** found a way through, with Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham again proving the difference — the pair have now scored in 10 of **England**'s last 11 tournament wins, a staggering record of big-game consistency that Gareth Southgate's successor continues to lean on heavily.

**Congo DR** depart Football 2026 with their heads held high after a gutsy performance on the grandest stage they have ever reached. **England**, meanwhile, advance and face a mouthwatering Round of 16 clash against **Mexico** — on **Mexico**'s own turf. That match takes place on **Sunday, July 5 at 5:00 PM PT** at the iconic **Estadio Azteca in Mexico City**, where the atmosphere promises to be anything but neutral for the English contingent.
